name: Baby Beach
description: Named for pool-like calm water — toddlers can splash safely in the lagoon interior. Snorkeling improves near the left rock barrier: parrotfish, trunkfish, and sea turtles.

rockiness.beach: White sand inside the protected lagoon 🏖️
rockiness.ocean_floor: Sandy lagoon bottom; reef and rocks at the left rock barrier 🪸

live_rating.water_quality_note: Provisional estimate — not lab-tested; live water quality later

characteristics.depth_profile: Enclosed lagoon: ankle to waist-deep for a very long distance inside the natural reef breakwater. Depth increases near the left-side rock barrier where reef fish gather; do not swim beyond protected areas — open ocean side can be dangerous.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Rock barrier defines the lagoon; sandy bottom inside; reef and rocks at the channel edges
characteristics.crowds: moderate to busy on weekends; arrive early
characteristics.access: Free large parking lot; ~45 min drive from Palm Beach through San Nicolas
characteristics.facilities: Two beach restaurants, restrooms, snorkel gear rentals
fun_facts: Named for pool-like calm water — toddlers can splash safely in the lagoon interior.
Snorkeling improves near the left rock barrier: parrotfish, trunkfish, and sea turtles.
Drive passes San Nicolas street murals — worth combining with a south-coast culture stop.
safety_notes: Stay inside the protected lagoon; outer reef openings can have strong current and surge.
